_comment: Config for the geo-aware hero on the landing page. FLOW: one of 'welcome' types in, erases, then the location line (a matching 'rule' or a 'default') types in as the big opener, then an 'invite' fades in. Rules are checked top-to-bottom, FIRST match wins — put specific rules (a city) above broad ones. Only speak about location in a 'rule' where you have a SPECIFIC, personal story (never pin the exact detected city — IP geo is often wrong/off). When no rule matches, a random 'generic' line shows instead, which says NOTHING about location so it reads fine to anyone. Go easy on em dashes; prefer periods/commas. Placeholders: {city}, {region}, {country}. Match fields: cities[] (case-insensitive), regionCodes[] (state code like HI/MO, assumed US unless you add "country":"XX"), countryCodes[] (US, GB...). Loaded by lib/greetingConfig.ts.
welcome: Welcome. I’m glad you found your way here.
Come in. You caught me mid-thought.
identity: CS + Math @ WashU, focused on distributed systems and robotics. prev SWE @ Tesla, Capital One, and Spectrum.

rules.say: Hawaii? So am I. O’ahu, Roosevelt High, Rough Rider through and through.
Looks like you’re near my current corner of the world. These days you’ll find me around WashU.
The Bay, maybe? I pass through more than you’d think. Coffee’s on me sometime.
New York. One of my favorite places to lose a whole weekend.
Pacific Northwest, if I had to guess. Good coffee, good code.

generic: You’ve wandered into a quiet corner of my mind.
Everything here is something I couldn’t stop thinking about.
However you found this place, welcome to the inside of my head.
invites: Wander as far in as you’d like.
Stay a while. It opens up the deeper you go.
Take your time. I built it to be explored.
